instructions.instruction: Configure @nx-bun/nx executor targets (build, serve, test) in a project.json using the correct executor identifiers and declare outputs for cacheable targets.
instructions.relevant_when: Adding or converting a project to use Bun executors in an Nx workspace.
instructions.why_given: The skill specifies the exact executor names (@nx-bun/nx:build, @nx-bun/nx:run, @nx-bun/nx:test) and requires outputs to be declared for cache reliability.

instructions.instruction: Never configure both Jest and @nx-bun/nx:test on the same test files; split test ownership by project and decommission the old test target.
instructions.relevant_when: Converting an existing project that has a Jest test target to use the Bun test runner.
instructions.why_given: The skill prohibits dual test ownership as it creates inconsistent results and CI noise.

instructions.instruction: Set cache: true on build and test targets and declare outputs referencing {options.outputPath} or a workspace-relative coverage path.
instructions.relevant_when: Ensuring Nx can cache Bun build and test results.
instructions.why_given: The skill explicitly states that skipping cache metadata reduces cache hit reliability.

instructions.instruction: After converting a project to Bun, remove any leftover npm or yarn lockfiles for the migrated scope to prevent resolver drift.
instructions.relevant_when: Migrating a project from Node.js/npm to Bun runtime.
instructions.why_given: The skill warns that mixed lockfiles and resolvers cause non-reproducible installs.
